name: agent-experts
goal: Refactor BUILD mode agent infrastructure from monolithic context (~700+ lines, triple duplication) to a two-dimensional composition model: expertise profiles + roles. Planner context drops to <150 lines; subagents get focused, relevant context via a 4-layer stack.

userStories.title: Move Build Skills to .pi/skills/
userStories.description: Move build skills from .agents/skills/ to .pi/skills/ (replacing symlinks with real files). Update plan-mode extension, DEVELOPER.md, and all references.
acceptanceCriteria: All 7 build skills exist as real files (not symlinks) in .pi/skills/
.agents/skills/ directory no longer exists
grep -r 'agents/skills' .pi/extensions/ returns nothing
npm run typecheck passes
Pi discovers skills correctly (available_skills show .pi/skills/ paths)

userStories.title: Create build-standards.md
userStories.description: Consolidate all coding standards, testing requirements, and code review checklists from three duplicated sources (conventions.md, testing.mdc, APPEND_SYSTEM.md) into a single .pi/standards/build-standards.md. Remove duplicated content from role files.
acceptanceCriteria: .pi/standards/build-standards.md exists and is <500 lines
Contains all coding standards, testing rules, quality gates, code review checklist, commit format
No coding standards duplicated in developer.md or reviewer.md
Content from all three sources is captured (nothing lost)

userStories.title: Rewrite APPEND_SYSTEM.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Rewrite both planner context files together with clear content boundary. AGENTS.md = 'what is available?', APPEND_SYSTEM.md = 'how should I work?'. Remove BUILD pipeline, delete .agents/sources/builder/.
acceptanceCriteria: AGENTS.md is <100 lines, hand-written (no generation pipeline)
APPEND_SYSTEM.md is <100 lines
Neither file contains coding conventions, testing rules, or code review checklists
No content duplication between the two files
npm run build succeeds and dist/AGENTS.md contains GUIDE content
npm test passes
.agents/sources/builder/ directory no longer exists
build:agents:dev script no longer exists in package.json

userStories.title: Create Core Expertise Profile
userStories.description: Write .pi/expertise/core/PROFILE.md — the domain map for packages/core/. CRITICAL: Must read actual source files before writing. Covers all services, search, integrations, adapters, storage.
acceptanceCriteria: .pi/expertise/core/PROFILE.md exists, ~200-250 lines
Accurately describes the architecture (spot-check 3 claims verified against source)
An agent reading it can identify which service to modify for a given task
Cross-references CLI expertise profile
Lists all LEARNINGS.md locations in core

userStories.title: Create CLI Expertise Profile
userStories.description: Write .pi/expertise/cli/PROFILE.md — the domain map for packages/cli/. CRITICAL: Must read actual source files before writing. Maps each CLI command to the core service it consumes.
acceptanceCriteria: .pi/expertise/cli/PROFILE.md exists, ~200-250 lines
Accurately maps CLI commands to core services (spot-check 3 claims verified)
An agent reading it knows where CLI meets core
Cross-references core expertise profile

userStories.title: Update Role Definitions for Composition
userStories.description: Add composition instructions to each role file (developer, reviewer, orchestrator, engineering-lead, product-manager). Add build-standards.md reference and maintenance checklist. Remove only clearly duplicated coding standards.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 role files have a Composition section
All 5 role files reference build-standards.md
No coding standards duplicated between roles and build-standards.md
Core behavioral sections of each role remain intact
All role files have maintenance checklist

userStories.title: Update Orchestrator for Expertise-Aware Spawning
userStories.description: Update orchestrator.md and execute-prd SKILL.md to compose subagent context with the right expertise profile(s) based on task area.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator.md describes how to determine which expertise to attach to a task
Orchestrator.md describes the 4-layer composition model
execute-prd SKILL.md includes guidance for expertise-aware spawning
An orchestrator following these instructions would attach core expertise to core tasks and cli expertise to CLI tasks

userStories.title: Delete Deprecated Files and Update References
userStories.description: Delete .cursor/rules/ directory. Update DEVELOPER.md, SETUP.md, and capabilities.json. Clean up all remaining stale references.
acceptanceCriteria: .cursor/rules/ directory no longer exists
No references to .cursor/rules/ in DEVELOPER.md, SETUP.md, or capabilities.json
All capabilities.json paths in implementationPaths and readBeforeChange point to files that exist
No broken references in active documentation (archive files excluded)

userStories.title: Create Smoke Test Document
userStories.description: Write smoke-tests.md with 8-10 test scenarios to manually validate the new agent composition model across all agent types.
acceptanceCriteria: smoke-tests.md exists with >=8 test scenarios
Each scenario has: prompt, expected behavior, red flags
Scenarios cover all agent types: planner, developer, reviewer, engineering-lead, orchestrator
Scenarios test both single-expertise and multi-expertise cases
